label: no
cot_prefix: Okay, let me try to figure out this locker problem. So, there are 100 lockers, all starting closed. Then there are 100 people, each person k toggles every k-th locker. Toggling means if it's open, close it, and if it's closed, open it. Person 1 opens all lockers because they toggle every first locker, which are all closed initially. Then Person 2 toggles every 2nd locker, so lockers 2, 4, 6, etc. But since they started open (from Person 1), Person 2 will close them. Then Person 3 toggles every 3rd locker, and so on up to Person 100. Hmm, the question is how many lockers are open at the end. I need to figure out for each locker how many times it's toggled. Because if it's toggled an even number of times, it will end up closed (since it starts closed), and if it's toggled an odd number of times, it will be open. So, for each locker number, say locker number
